meta.title: Drift — Nomad Super App
meta.subtitle: A crypto-native mobile app that collapses five separate tools into one scroll — city discovery, remote jobs, and IRL community for the location-independent generation.

data.headline: Five apps to answer one question: should I move here?
data.body: Every time a digital nomad considers a new city, they open the same five tabs. Nomad List for cost and livability. A job board for remote work. Meetup or Telegram for community. Google Maps for vibe. It works — but it's fragmented, slow, and exhausting. Drift is a single app that answers all three: Can I afford it? Can I work from it? Will I belong there?

data.label: to decide on one city
data.body: The average crypto-earning nomad context-switches between five separate tools before committing to a relocation. Drift collapses the stack. One scroll replaces the whole session.

data.text: I know the job market's good in Prague. I know I can afford it. I just don't know if I'll be eating alone.
data.context: Web3 freelancer · Bali → considering Prague — composite user quote

items.title: Score it before you book the flight.
items.body: Livability score, monthly cost, number of nomads already there, and open remote jobs — everything needed to commit to a city, without leaving the app. Prague 9.2 · $3,200/mo · 99+ nomads.

items.title: Your stack, your rate. No middleman.
items.body: Job listings that accept USD, Euro, BTC, and ETH as payment options — treated as equals, not footnotes. For a generation that doesn't use bank transfers, this is the baseline.

items.title: 99 people already said yes.
items.body: Events aren't a feature — they're the answer to the loneliness problem. The nomad avatar stack isn't decoration. It's the most important signal on the screen: you won't be alone here.

items.title: Crypto payment as equals, not exceptions
items.description: Putting BTC and ETH on a job listing risks feeling sketchy — like a red flag for scam posts. The decision: render all four payment options (USD · Euro · BTC · ETH) as identical pill badges, same size, same weight, same position. No special icon. No asterisk. When crypto sits visually equal to fiat, it reads as a legitimate option, not an outlier.

items.title: Four numbers, not fifty
items.description: Nomad List has 50+ data points per city. The risk of replicating that: analysis paralysis. The decision: surface only four — livability score, monthly cost, current nomad count, and open job listings. These four map directly to the three decisions a nomad is trying to make. Everything else lives behind 'City info' tab, one tap deeper.

items.title: Social proof before event detail
items.description: On the event screen, '99+ nomads joined' appears above the event description — not below it. The decision was deliberate: a nomad decides whether to engage with an event based on who's going, not what it is. Show the crowd first. Let the content close the deal.

items.name: Alex Chen
items.biography: Frontend developer turned product designer, Alex earns in USDC and ETH. Lives on iPhone and a MacBook in cafés. Finds community through Twitter, Telegram nomad groups, and Bitcoin meetups. Has moved six times in two years — always to cities with a crypto scene.
items.goal: Land a well-paid remote contract, move to a city with a strong nomad presence, and meet people worth knowing within the first two weeks.
items.needs: City livability data and job market signals in one place. Crypto payment options as a first-class feature, not a workaround. A way to find IRL events before landing.
items.frustrations: Switches between five apps to make one relocation decision. Most job boards treat crypto payment as a quirky edge case. Discord and Telegram groups are noisy and hard to trust.
items.why_use: Drift is the only app that treats the crypto-native, location-independent lifestyle as the default — not a niche.

data.text: The best relocation tool isn't a checklist. It's knowing someone will be at the coffee shop.
